gates.title: Packaged runtimes stop poisoning the core-artifact manifest with personal scripts during update
evidence_required: Packaged updates rebuild runtime-core-artifacts.json from the canonical npm package src tree instead of scanning ~/.nexo/scripts
Script classification prefers the canonical npm package source when available so a bad 5.3.8 manifest no longer hides personal scripts
Runtime doctor syncs personal scripts before LaunchAgent inventory so personal automations recover ownership cleanly after update

gates.title: Portable export/import continues to work after the manifest heal
evidence_required: nexo export still writes the portable bundle successfully on real packaged installs
Personal scripts remain included instead of disappearing into the core bucket
Regression tests cover the packaged manifest heal and script-registry recovery path

gates.title: Package, docs, plugin artifacts, and website tell the same v5.3.9 story
evidence_required: Root package and integration artifacts all point to 5.3.9
README, changelog, llms, and release contract mention the packaged core-artifact manifest heal clearly
Website home, blog, changelog, llms, and sitemap are updated to 5.3.9
